process.title: Our Process
process.subtitle: From Drying to Export: The Journey of Coffee Transformation

harvesting.title: Harvesting
harvesting.description: The journey begins with careful harvesting of ripe coffee cherries. Our partners select only the finest cherries, ensuring optimal flavor development and quality from the very start. Each cherry is hand-picked at peak ripeness to guarantee the best possible foundation for exceptional coffee.

drying.title: Drying
drying.description: Freshly harvested coffee cherries are spread on drying beds where they're regularly turned to ensure even drying. This crucial step reduces moisture content to precisely 11-12% while preserving the beans' intrinsic flavors. The process typically takes 15-21 days depending on climate conditions.

hulling.title: Hulling
hulling.description: Our specialized hulling machines gently remove the parchment layer surrounding the coffee beans. This process is carefully calibrated to prevent damage to the beans while efficiently removing the outer layer. Our equipment can process up to 2 tons of coffee per hour while maintaining exceptional quality standards.

sorting.title: Sorting
sorting.description: Using semi-automated sorting machines, we separate beans by size, density, and color. This precision sorting ensures consistency and removes any defective beans that could affect the final quality. Our optical sorters can detect and remove up to 99.9% of defective beans, ensuring only the finest make it to the next stage.

grading.title: Quality Control
grading.description: Expert quality controllers assess the beans based on international standards. Each batch undergoes rigorous testing for moisture content, defect count, and cup quality to ensure only the finest beans proceed. Our Q-graders evaluate every lot using the Specialty Coffee Association protocols to assign accurate quality scores.

packaging.title: Packaging
packaging.description: The graded beans are carefully packaged in specialized GrainPro or jute bags that preserve freshness and protect against environmental factors. Each package is labeled with detailed information about origin, grade, processing method, and lot number for complete traceability from farm to cup.

export.title: Export Preparation
export.description: The final step involves preparing all necessary documentation, arranging logistics, and ensuring compliance with international regulations. Our export facilitation services handle every detail for a smooth journey to global markets, including customs clearance, shipping arrangements, and all required certifications.

partners.title: Who We Work With
partners.subtitle: Our trusted partners in the global coffee industry

fairtrade.description: A global organization working to secure a better deal for farmers and workers.

flocert.description: A global certification and verification body, with the main role of independently certifying Fairtrade products.

gcp.description: An inclusive multi-stakeholder sustainability platform aligning the activities towards creating a thriving and sustainable coffee sector.

afca.description: A regional non profit, non political, member-driven association representing coffee sectors in 11 countries.

rainforest.description: An international nonprofit organization that works to conserve biodiversity and ensure sustainable livelihoods.

utz.description: A worldwide certification program for responsible coffee and other products.

about.title: About Cafe Kivu Congo
about.subtitle: Transforming the coffee industry in Congo with sustainable practices and exceptional quality

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: At Cafe Kivu Congo, we're dedicated to transforming the coffee industry in Congo through sustainable practices, fair partnerships, and exceptional quality standards.
mission.content1: We are committed to maintaining the highest standards of quality throughout the entire coffee transformation process, from bean selection to final export.
mission.content2: Our goal is to position Congolese coffee on the global stage, creating economic opportunities for local communities while delivering exceptional products to the world.
mission.content3: We believe in responsible practices that protect our environment, support our farmers, and ensure the long-term sustainability of the coffee industry in Congo.

values.title: Our Values
values.subtitle: The principles that guide everything we do at Cafe Kivu Congo

quality.title: Quality
quality.description: We are uncompromising in our commitment to excellence at every stage of the coffee transformation process.

integrity.title: Integrity
integrity.description: We conduct our business with honesty, transparency, and ethical practices that build trust with all stakeholders.

community.title: Community
community.description: We value the well-being of the communities we work with and strive to create positive social impact.

sustainability.title: Sustainability
sustainability.description: We implement environmentally responsible practices that protect the ecosystems where our coffee is grown.

fairness.title: Fairness
fairness.description: We believe in equitable relationships with farmers and partners, ensuring fair compensation and treatment.

excellence.title: Excellence
excellence.description: We continuously improve our processes, skills, and knowledge to deliver the best possible products and services.

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: Meet the passionate professionals behind Cafe Kivu Congo

member1.name: Jean-Pierre Muhindo
member1.bio: With over 20 years of experience in the coffee industry, Jean-Pierre founded Cafe Kivu Congo with a vision to transform the local coffee sector.

member2.name: Marie Kabila
member2.bio: Marie oversees all operational aspects of our business, ensuring efficiency and quality at every stage of the transformation process.

member3.name: Emmanuel Nkunda
member3.bio: A certified Q Grader, Emmanuel leads our quality control team with his exceptional palate and attention to detail.

member4.name: Sophie Mutombo
member4.bio: Sophie develops and implements our sustainability initiatives, working closely with farmers to promote environmentally friendly practices.
